steps.title: Calculate the sample mean
steps.content:
Add all five tree counts and divide by 5:
Sum = 51 + 59 + 45 + 52 + 73 = 280
Mean = 280 ÷ 5 = 56 trees per acre

steps.title: Estimate total trees in the forest
steps.content:
Multiply the sample mean by the total forest size:
$$56 \text{ trees/acre} \times 253 \text{ acres}$$
Break it into parts: $56 \times 250 = 14,000$ and $56 \times 3 = 168$
Total estimate = 14,000 + 168 = 14,168 trees

steps.title: Identify the matching range
steps.content:
Check which range contains 14,168:
  • A: 11,000–12,000 — too low
  • B: 12,500–13,500 — too low
  • C: 13,500–14,500 — includes 14,168 ✓
  • D: 18,000–19,000 — too high
That matches choice C — C is correct.
